review_command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --tools Read,Grep,Glob --permission-mode dontAsk --output-format json

findings.title: Optimistic completion_gates labels before final closeout artifacts
findings.summary: Contract completion_gates marked Claude cross-validation and production readiness as passed while review-time evidence text still described artifacts that would be recorded before production_ready promotion.

findings.title: Pre-existing weighted-ledger Hermes/Claude plan-ref loop coverage gap
findings.summary: Validator plan-ref coverage loop stops before M04.S04 and M05.S01; this does not affect HumanApproval package-layout correctness and is accepted for a future maintenance pass.

findings.title: Cosmetic key-name drift between packet and contract lifecycle wording
findings.summary: Packet uses does_not_implement_HumanApproval_lifecycle_status while contract uses does_not_implement_HumanApproval_lifecycle; validator reads the contract keys and the packet key is harmless.

P2.title: Optimistic completion_gates labels before final closeout artifacts
P2.resolution: Completion gate evidence text is updated during production_ready promotion to describe completed Claude review, adjudication, construction inspection, and final validation evidence rather than future-tense pre-promotion dependency text.

P3.title: Weighted-ledger Hermes/Claude plan-ref loop coverage gap
P3.resolution: Accepted as pre-existing validator completeness work; not required for this package-layout closeout because H00/C00 refs and command evidence are enforced through the current subphase contract and closeout artifacts.

P3.title: Cosmetic packet/contract lifecycle key-name drift
P3.resolution: No validator or runtime behavior depends on the packet's cosmetic boundary key name; contract scope policy remains authoritative.

followup_review_reason: No P0/P1 blockers. The single P2 is resolved by expected closeout evidence promotion text, with no post-review behavioral code change required.
